label: Regret Minimisation
intent: User faces a significant fork-in-the-road decision and is stuck between options
protocol: Project to age 80 looking back: for each option, ask 'Would the 80-year-old version of you regret not doing this?'
Explore both directions: regret of action (doing it and having it go wrong) vs regret of inaction (not doing it at all)
Identify what each option protects or sacrifices
Separate reversible from irreversible decisions: 'Is this as permanent as it feels?'
Return to values: which option is more aligned with what the user named as important?
opening_moves: Imagine you're 80, looking back on this moment. Which choice do you think you'd regret not having made?
Is there a version of this choice that feels more like you?
deepening_questions: What would you lose if you chose option A? What would you protect?
What would you lose if you chose option B? What would you gain?
Is this decision as irreversible as it feels right now?
If the 80-year-old version of you were advising you right now, what would they say?
do_not: Do not choose for the user
Do not let the session collapse into pros/cons — it is a values and regret exercise, not a balance sheet
Do not treat the 80-year-old frame as purely analytical — it is meant to cut through short-term fear
exit_criteria: The user identifies which option the 80-year-old version of them would clearly endorse, even if they don't act on it immediately
